LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BANK OF THE WEST, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION. THE COURT DENIED THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION BUT DEFERS ENTERING AN ORDER PENDING THE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT GRANTS LEAVE TO AMEND ON THE CONDITION THAT PLAINTIFF PAY THE DEFENDANT THE SUM OF $1,000 DOLLARS. PLAINTIFF HAS TEN DAYS TO FILE SUCH AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT SHOULD NOT CONTAIN ALLEGATIONS DATING TO THE PERIOD PRIOR TO MS. COHEN'S FULL-TIME EMPLOYMENT BY BANK OF THE WEST COMMENCING IN OCTOBER OF 2017. CONCURRENTY, PLAINTIFF IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER DENYING THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. IF THE DEFENDANT, HAS ANY OBJECTIONS TO THE COURT'S DENYING THE MOTION, BASED ON THE CONTENTS OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT, COUNSEL SHOULD PROMPTLY NOTIFY THE COURT AND THE PLAINTIFF AND THE COURT WILL HOLD A FURTHER HEARING, OTHERWISE THE COURT WILL ENTER THE ORDER DENYING THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER (CONCURRENT WITH THE FILING OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT) THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: FURTHER HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S PENDING MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS OFF CALENDAR. THE COURT'S JUNE 13, 2019 ORDER GRANTED PLAINTIFF 10 DAYS LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT, AND INDICATED THAT IT WOULD DENY THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DID NOT CONTAIN ALLEGATIONS DATING TO THE PERIOD PRIOR TO PLAINTIFF'S FULL-TIME EMPLOYMENT BY BANK OF THE WEST COMMENCING IN OCTOBER OF 2017. WHILE DEFENDANT HAS FILED AN OBJECTION TO THE PROPOSED ORDER, NO AMENDED COMPLAINT HAS BEEN FILED OR RECEIVED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-22-2019 Set For Trial On Jun-29-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Bank Of The West'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Sep-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matt Wayne, Esq. 415-331-4400 of Armstrong Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Brian Ashe, Esq. 415-397-2823 of Seyfarth Shaw for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Bank Of The West'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action.

Defendant Bank of the West, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and to stay action is denied. The scope of the arbitration clause in the April 27, 2017 Contingent Worker Agreement between plaintiff and Matrix Resources, Inc. was limited to "any dispute that arises out of or relates to [plaintiff's] assignment with the Bank." That assignment terminated on October 23, 2017, when plaintiff accepted an offer of employment extended to her by Bank of the West. (Cohen Decl., Ex. B.) That offer contained an express integration clause in which plaintiff acknowledged, "there are no other agreements or terms other than what is set forth in this letter, and that the provisions of this offer letter supersede any prior representations or agreements that might have been made between Bank of the West and myself. I further acknowledge that this letter constitutes all of the terms of the offer and there are no other agreements expanding or modifying the terms of this letter." (Id.) All of the claims asserted by plaintiff in her first amended complaint for damages dated August 13, 2019 relate to the period of her employment by Bank of the West. Thus, there is no arbitration agreement between plaintiff and Bank of the West to arbitrate claims arising out of her employment, and Bank of the West cannot compel those claims to arbitration under the Matrix agreement. "'The strong public policy in favor of arbitration does not extend to those who are not parties to an arbitration agreement, and a party cannot be compelled to arbitrate a dispute that he has not agreed to resolve by arbitration.'" (Buckner v. Tamarin (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 140, 142.)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Further Hearing On Defendant'S Pending Motion To Compel Arbitration (Email Dated 7/22/19)
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

There being no appearance or objection, the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Further Hearing On Defendant's Pending Motion To Compel Arbitration is off calendar.

The Court's June 13, 2019 order granted plaintiff 10 days leave to file an amended complaint, and indicated that it would deny the motion to compel arbitration if the amended complaint did not contain allegations dating to the period prior to Plaintiff's full-time employment by Bank of the West commencing in October of 2017. While defendant has filed an objection to the proposed order, no amended complaint has been filed or received by the Court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Defendant Bank Of The West, Inc. Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action (Tentative Ruling Only Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar Jun-13-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Jun-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matthew Wayne, Esq. 415-331-4400 pf the Armstron Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Brian Ashe, Esq. 415-397-2823 for Defendant BANK OF THE WEST, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. In addition to the Court's tentative ruling, the Court shares its views. Plaintiff Argues and requests orally to amend the complaint. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BANK OF THE WEST, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action.

The Court denied the motion to compel arbitration but defers entering an order pending the filing of an amended complaint. The Court grants leave to amend on the condition that Plaintiff pay the Defendant the sum of $1,000 dollars. Plaintiff has ten days to file such an amended complaint. The amended Complaint should not contain allegations dating to the period prior to Ms. Cohen's full-time employment by Bank of the West commencing in October of 2017. Concurrenty, Plaintiff is to submit a proposed order denying the motion to compel arbitration. If the defendant, has any objections to the Court's denying the motion, based on the contents of the amended complaint, Counsel should promptly notify the Court and the Plaintiff and the Court will hold a further hearing, otherwise the Court will enter the order denying the motion.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order (Concurrent with the filing of the amended complaint) that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)
